Ukraine Conflict News





23 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Lethal Kinetic Strike, Jan. 23, 2026 SOUTHCOM 23 Jan 2026 -- On Jan. 23, at the direction of Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations.

  • Venezuela has right to choose partners, Chinese FM on US official's claim of not allowing 'undercut' oil prices Global Times 23 Jan 2026 -- Venezuela is a sovereign country and has the right to independently choose its cooperation partners, China's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday, when asked to comment on that a US official claimed on Thursday that the US government would allow China to purchase Venezuelan oil but not at the "undercut" prices at which crude oil was sold during the Maduro administration.
  • U.S. House blocks resolution to bar Trump from using military force in Venezuela People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- The U.S. House of Representatives on Thursday narrowly rejected a Democratic-led resolution barring President Donald Trump from further using military force in Venezuela without explicit congressional authorization.

  • Connecticut Guard Joint Staff Trains for Emergency Response as Winter Storm Arrives Army News 23 Jan 2026 -- Members of the Connecticut National Guard's Joint Force Headquarters joint staff completed a five-day Joint Task Force Staff Training Course at Camp Nett, strengthening their ability to plan and coordinate National Guard support during domestic emergency operations as a major winter storm threatened parts of the region.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Trump says U.S. wants "total access" to Greenland People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- The White House is negotiating a deal to secure U.S. "total access" to Denmark's Greenland with no time limit, President Donald Trump told Fox Business on Thursday.
  • Denmark says NATO chief has no mandate to negotiate on Greenland People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen stated on Thursday that NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte does not have a mandate to negotiate on behalf of Denmark or Greenland.
  • Concerns about U.S. armed seizure of Greenland ease, transatlantic rifts remain People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- A freewheeling speech by U.S. President Donald Trump in the Swiss Alpine town of Davos on Wednesday stunned the audience, putting transatlantic rifts under the spotlight, despite eased concerns about a possible U.S. armed seizure of Greenland, a self-governing territory of Denmark.

  • 2026 National Defense Strategy DOD 23 Jan 2026

  • Readout of Principal Deputy Assistant Secretary for Indo-Pacific Security Affairs Concludes Visit to Singapore DoD 23 Jan 2026
  • USAF to introduce Air Expeditionary Wing 2.0 US Air Force 23 Jan 2026 -- Beginning this year, the Air Force is introducing Air Expeditionary Wing 2.0, an improved version of our legacy deployment construct, as its new wing unit of action. AEW 2.0 will be implemented in fiscal year 2027.
  • U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star marks 50 years of service, begins Operation Deep Freeze 2026 US Coast Guard 23 Jan 2026 -- The U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Polar Star (WAGB 10) began icebreaking operations in the Southern Ocean in support of Operation Deep Freeze 2026 and marked its 50th year of commissioned service Saturday by freeing and escorting a cruise ship trapped in pack ice.
  • Connecticut Guard Home to Specialized Aircraft Maintenance Facility Army News 23 Jan 2026 -- At the Connecticut National Guard's 1109th Aviation Classification and Repair Depot, or AVCRAD, workers refurbish and maintain the U.S. Army's fleet of rotary-wing aircraft, a unique job that can save the Department of War, or DOW, millions of dollars a year.
  • Nevada Army Guard 321st Signal Company activated to support 17th Sustainment Brigade Army News 23 Jan 2026 -- The Nevada Army National Guard activated the 321st Signal Company during an uncasing ceremony in December, formally establishing a new signal unit assigned to the 17th Sustainment Brigade.

  • Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen: A new NATO accelerator site in Finland will combine technology, defence and economic growth Finnish MoD 23 Jan 2026 -- A new NATO accelerator site was inaugurated in Espoo, Finland, on 22 January 2026. Finland's Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen gave a speech at the event.
  • Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen's speech at the opening of Finland's NATO DIANA Innovation Accelerator Finnish MoD 23 Jan 2026 -- Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen held a speech at the opening gala of Finland's NATO DIANA Innovation Accelerator on 22 January 2026.

  • Russia Boosting Development of Its Icebreaker Fleet - Putin Sputnik 23 Jan 2026 -- Russia is increasing the power of its nuclear icebreakers, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Friday.

  • Mykhailo Fedorov and the U.S. Chargé d'Affaires discuss cooperation on air defense and drones Ukraine MOD 23 Jan 2026
  • Mykhailo Fedorov appoints Valeriya Ionan as adviser on international projects Ukraine MOD 23 Jan 2026
  • Operation Interflex: how allies train Ukrainian service personnel in the United Kingdom Ukraine MOD 23 Jan 2026
  • Share of Ukrainian manufacturers in military procurement rises to 82% Ukraine MOD 23 Jan 2026
  • Mykhailo Fedorov signs order to launch Mission Control drone command and control system in DELTA Ukraine MOD 23 Jan 2026

  • Legislature blocks special defense budget review despite U.S. pressure CNA 23 Jan 2026 -- The Legislature on Friday blocked the review of a Cabinet-proposed NT$1.25 trillion (US$39.6 billion) special defense budget, one day after American Institute in Taiwan (AIT) Director Raymond Greene commented that "freedom is not free."

  • Local defence manufacturing industry receives funding boost Australia DOD 23 Jan 2026 -- More than $17 million has been allocated to 44 Australian businesses in the latest rounds of the Defence Industry Development Grants (DIDG) program.
  • Heavy vehicles, lighter footprint Australia DOD 23 Jan 2026 -- Defence is trialling a relocatable vehicle wash point adapted by Australian mining industry experts.

  • OCCAR HORIZON MLU Milestone reached with KRONOS Grand Naval KGN Radar Installed at Shore Integration Facility SIF DGA SESDA OCCAR 23 Jan 2026 -- An important milestone for Horizon Mid-Life-Upgrade (MLU) Programme has been reached with the installation of the Kronos Grand Naval (KGN) radar at the Shore Integration Facility (SIF) in Saint-Mandrier (Direction Générale de l'Armement - DGA - Site d'Expérimentation des Systèmes de Défense Aérienne - SESDA)

  • Minister of National Defence R. Kaunas to see Federal Minister of Defence of Germany Lithuania MND 23 Jan 2026 -- On January 26 Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas will pay a visit to Berlin where he will meet with Federal Minister of Defence of Germany Boris Pistorius.
  • The Chief of Defence, at the MCCS in Brussels Romania - Ministry of National Defence 23 Jan 2026 -- On the second day of the NATO Military Committee in Chiefs of Defence Session (MCCS), the Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, participated in the sessions dedicated to cooperation with NATO partners.

  • PAF gets 5 more 'Black Hawk' helicopters Philippine News Agency 23 Jan 2026 -- The helicopter fleet of the Philippine Air Force (PAF) was further strengthened as the service formally accepted into service five more brand-new S-70i "Black Hawk" utility helicopters during a joint turnover and blessing ceremony at Villamor Air Base, Pasay City Friday.
  • Army developing more potent drone capabilities Philippine News Agency 23 Jan 2026 -- The Philippine Army (PA) will be developing more potent drone capabilities to help in its mission to secure the country, its spokesperson said on Friday.
  • AFP reiterates commitment to fighting extremism at all fronts Philippine News Agency 23 Jan 2026 -- With the conviction of the Tacloban court of two militant activists for terrorism financing, the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) on Friday reiterated its commitment to protecting Filipinos against all forms of terrorism and violent extremism.
  • PH eyes peacekeeping, defense collaboration with Germany Philippine News Agency 23 Jan 2026 -- The Philippines is exploring possible peacekeeping and defense industry cooperation with Germany as part of efforts to expand its security ties with friendly nations, the Department of National Defense (DND) has said.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Haitian Prime Minister Fils-Aimé US Dept. of State 23 Jan 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Colombian Foreign Minister Villavicencio US Dept. of State 23 Jan 2026

  • Experts slam 'scientifically reckless' US withdrawal from WHO Press TV 23 Jan 2026 -- The United States has completed its withdrawal from the World Health Organization, drawing sharp criticism from public health experts and leaving behind more than a quarter-billion dollars in unpaid dues.

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for Jan. 23, 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 23 January 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 23 Jan 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • US threat to Iran's Leader will ignite entire region: Hezbollah Press TV 23 Jan 2026 -- The political wing of the Hezbollah resistance movement in the Lebanese Parliament has warned that the United States' rhetorical threats against Iran and Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei risk escalating tensions in West Asia.
  • Trump disinvites Canada from Gaza board after PM Carney's blistering critique of US hegemony Press TV 23 Jan 2026 -- Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has been abruptly disinvited from US President Donald Trump's newly launched "Board of Peace," a move that has highlighted growing international unease over an initiative widely viewed as coercive and politically compromised.
  • Trump launches "Board of Peace" in Davos, drawing criticism for undermining UN People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump formally launched his so-called "Board of Peace" on Thursday at a charter-signing ceremony during the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland.
  • Trump withdraws invitation to Canadian PM to join Board of Peace People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday the Board of Peace he proposed is withdrawing its invitation to Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney for Canada to join the body.

  • EU has serious doubts about U.S.-proposed Board of Peace, says EU Council president People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- The European Union (EU) has serious doubts about the Board of Peace proposed by the United States and will defend itself against any form of coercion, European Council President Antonio Costa said after the council's emergency summit concluded shortly after midnight Thursday.
  • Britain won't sign Trump's "Board of Peace" treaty at signing ceremony: foreign secretary People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- Britain will not sign U.S. President Donald Trump's so-called "Board of Peace" treaty on Thursday, said British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper.
  • Russia willing to allocate 1 bln to Gaza Peace Board from U.S.-frozen assets People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- Russia is willing to allocate 1 billion U.S. dollars from Russia's U.S.-frozen assets to the Board of Peace for Gaza, a move that would require the unblocking of the assets, Kremlin Spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Thursday.

  • Gaza reconstruction talks must not distract from massive needs, say UN aid agencies UN News 23 Jan 2026 -- Amid reconstruction talks on Gaza linked to President Trump's just-launched Board of Peace, UN aid agencies insisted on Friday that what Gazans need most is immediate relief from the ongoing humanitarian catastrophe there.
  • Demolition of UNRWA premises is symbolic of Israel's attack on the United Nations system: UN expert OHCHR 23 Jan 2026 -- The UN Special Rapporteur on the occupied Palestinian territory, Francesca Albanese, today expressed grave concern about the ongoing, systematic attack against the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA), the UN system and the Palestinian people, after Israeli forces forcibly entered and began demolishing the UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem this week. Albanese is calling on the General Assembly to convene a special session to consider next steps after these unprecedented attacks on UNRWA and the wider UN. She issued the following statement:

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Afghanistan, Nigeria, Syria, Mozambique, UNOCHA 23 Jan 2026

  • 2 Filipinos dead as cargo ship capsizes near Scarborough Shoal Philippine News Agency 23 Jan 2026 -- Two of the 21 Filipino crew members aboard a Singaporean-flagged cargo vessel that capsized near Scarborough Shoal have been confirmed dead, while search and rescue operations continue for those still missing.
  • PCG deploys ships, aircraft to search, rescue Filipino mariners in WPS Philippine News Agency 23 Jan 2026 -- The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) on Friday dispatched two vessels and two aircraft for the search and rescue (SAR) of the remaining missing Filipino crew of a capsized foreign cargo vessel in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) before dawn on Friday.

  • Limited access restored to Syria's Al Hol camp amid security concerns UN News 23 Jan 2026 -- United Nations agencies have regained limited access to Syria's Al Hol camp and resumed the delivery of essential supplies after days of disruption caused by a volatile security situation, the UN Deputy Spokesperson said on Friday.

  • Anger And Chaos As Thousands Displaced Ahead Of Pakistani Military Operation RFE/RL 23 Jan 2026 -- Tens of thousands of people traveling in crammed cars and on packed buses have left a remote valley in northwestern Pakistan ahead of a planned military operation against suspected militants active in the area.

  • 10 army troopers killed, 10 injured as vehicle falls into gorge in India-controlled Kashmir People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- At least 10 Indian army troopers were killed and 10 others injured Thursday after a vehicle carrying them skidded off the mountainous road and fell into a gorge in Indian-controlled Kashmir, officials said.

  • "Illegitimate elections yield illegitimate results": UN expert urges rejection of Myanmar junta's sham poll results OHCHR 23 Jan 2026 -- The international community must unequivocally reject as illegitimate the election results in Myanmar and any power arrangement that follows, a UN expert said today, as voting concludes in the country's discredited parliamentary polls this weekend.

  • Army chief condemns ambush of 4 soldiers in Lanao Norte Philippine News Agency 23 Jan 2026 -- Philippine Army (PA) commander Lt. Gen. Antonio Nafarrete condemned the "heinous and cowardly" ambush by Dawlah Islamiyah-Maute Group (DI-MG) terrorists that killed four government troops in Munai, Lanao del Norte this Friday.

  • UN envoy highlights 'window to advance peace' in Colombia UN News 23 Jan 2026 -- The new year provides an opportunity for Colombia to keep advancing towards lasting peace, a decade after a landmark accord that ended more than 50 years of conflict, the UN Special Representative told the Security Council on Friday.
  • Colombia Must Protect Former Combatants and Speed Rural Reform as Peace Agreement Reaches 10 Years, Special Representative Tells Security Council UN Security Council 23 Jan 2026 -- As the 2016 peace agreement in Colombia reaches the tenth year of its 15-year timeline, the Head of the UN Mission in that country called on its Government to continue to protect former combatants who have laid down arms, accelerate rural land reform and prepare for milestone elections in 2026.
  • The UK urges full implementation of Colombia's 2016 Peace Agreement: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 23 Jan 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Colombia.

News Reports

  • USA migrant crackdown: UN Human Rights Chief decries dehumanisation, harmful policies and practices OHCHR 23 Jan 2026 -- UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk on Friday called on the United States to ensure that its migration policies and enforcement practices respect human dignity and due process rights, decrying the dehumanising portrayal and harmful treatment of migrants and refugees.
  • UN rights chief decries US treatment of migrants, as deaths in ICE custody rise UN News 23 Jan 2026 -- The UN's top human rights official has raised alarm over what he described as the growing dehumanisation of migrants in the United States, warning that current immigration enforcement practices are undermining due process, family unity and basic human dignity.

  • Countries progress negotiations in support of WHO Pandemic Agreement WHO 23 Jan 2026

  • Money laundering and terrorist financing progress reports on Azerbaijan, Croatia and Estonia Council of Europe 23 Jan 2026 -- The Council of Europe anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism financing body (MONEYVAL) has published follow-up reports on Azerbaijan, Croatia and Estonia, reassessing their technical compliance with a number of the 40 Financial action task force (FATF) recommendations.

  • Commentary by Aide to the President Yury Ushakov regarding Vladimir Putin's meeting with US President's Special Envoy Steve Witkoff President of Russia 23 Jan 2026 -- Aide to the President Yury Ushakov: President Vladimir Putin has held a meeting with US President's Special Envoy Steve Witkoff. Jared Kushner and Josh Gruenbaum also attended the meeting. The latter is a White House Senior Advisor who specialises in economic matters. This was the first time he joined the US team.
  • Russia-US Talks in the Kremlin were 'Constructive and Extremely Frank' — Kremlin Aide Ushakov Sputnik 23 Jan 2026 -- Kremlin meeting with Trump's special envoy Steve Witkoff, Jared Kushner, and Josh Gruenbaum lasted for more than 3.5 hours.
  • Russia, China Jointly Oppose West's Efforts to Militarize Asia-Pacific - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 23 Jan 2026 -- Russia and China jointly oppose attempts by Western countries to militarize the Asia-Pacific region and deploy their military infrastructure there, the Russian Foreign Ministry said on Friday.
  • Venezuela, Russia Reaffirm Commitment to Strategic Cooperation Sputnik 23 Jan 2026 -- On January 3, the US launched a massive attack on Venezuela, capturing President Nicolas Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, and taking them to New York.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on January 23, 2026 PRC MofA 23 Jan 2026
  • 'Head-of-state diplomacy plays an irreplaceable leading role in China-US relations': Chinese FM on Trump's claims to visit China in April Global Times 23 Jan 2026 -- In response to a question asking whether the Chinese side could confirm the relevant visits as media reported that the US President Donald Trump said on Thursday local time on Air Force One that he will visit China in April, Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday that the steady development of China-US relations serves the common interests of the peoples of both countries and meets the shared expectations of the international community.
  • President Xi Jinping Speaks with Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on the Phone PRC MofA 23 Jan 2026 -- On the morning of January 23, 2026, President Xi Jinping spoke with Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on the phone.
  • Central govt's authority, HKSAR govt oppose latest resolution by European Parliament on prosecution of Jimmy Lai Global Times 23 Jan 2026 -- The Commissioner's Office of Chinese Foreign Ministry in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) and the HKSAR government voiced opposition against a latest resolution adopted by European Parliament on Thursday local time on the prosecution of anti-government instigator and secessionist Jimmy Lai.
  • Chinese mission to EU expresses strong dissatisfaction over EP reports' China-related content Global Times 23 Jan 2026 -- When asked to comment on that the European Parliament on Wednesday adopted the Implementation of the common foreign and security policy - Annual report 2025 and the Implementation of the common security and defence policy - Annual report 2025, which contain multiple references to China, the spokesperson of the Chinese Mission said on Friday that the relevant reports of the European Parliament disregard facts, distort the truth, and crudely interfere in China's internal affairs on issues related to the Taiwan region, Hong Kong, the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region, the Xizang Autonomous Region, and the South China Sea. China expresses strong dissatisfaction and firm opposition to this, according to an official release from the Chinese Mission to the EU.
  • China's efforts in climate response, renewable energy widely recognized: FM spokesperson People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- As a responsible major developing country, China's endeavors in climate response and promoting global development and application of renewable energy are widely recognized, Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Thursday.
  • HKSAR govt opposes latest resolution by European Parliament on prosecution of Jimmy Lai Global Times 23 Jan 2026 -- The Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) government strongly opposed on late Thursday the so-called resolution adopted by the European Parliament against Hong Kong, condemning the Parliament for using the Lai Chee-ying case to make baseless allegations about Hong Kong and smear the Hong Kong National Security Law (HKNSL) and the Safeguarding National Security Ordinance (SNSO).

  • President urges rejection of China's red lines, calls for Taiwan-U.S. tax pact CNA 23 Jan 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Friday urged the international community not to allow China to unilaterally draw red lines that undermine regional stability, while calling for swift passage of legislation to resolve double taxation between Taiwan and the United States.
  • Taiwan will not aid aggressors, President Lai says after Zelensky remarks CNA 23 Jan 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Friday said Taiwan will not tolerate any assistance to aggressors or violations of international embargoes, following Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's recent remarks about the country.
  • Ex-President Tsai's think tank releases inaugural report on China CNA 23 Jan 2026 -- Thinking Taiwan, a think tank founded by former President Tsai Ing-wen (蔡英文), on Friday released its inaugural report on China, providing what it describes as a comprehensive framework to track Beijing's evolving political and economic trajectory.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 23 Jan 2026

  • President pledges revival of NK ties, 2018 inter-Korean pact KOREA.net 23 Jan 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on Jan. 21 said, "We will strive to restore the September 19 (2018) military agreement to prevent accidental clashes and build political and military trust between the two Koreas."

  • Trump Says Many US Warships Moving Toward Iran 'Just in Case' Sputnik 23 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said that a large number of US military ships are moving toward Iran "just in case."
  • Sanctions on Illicit Petroleum Traders to Support the People of Iran US Dept. of State 23 Jan 2026 -- Today, the United States is taking further action to deny the Iranian regime the resources to oppress its people.
  • Treasury Escalates Pressure on Iranian Regime for Killing Peaceful Protestors US Treasury 23 Jan 2026 -- Today, amidst the Iranian regime's brutal crackdown on peaceful protestors and its complete shutdown of internet access to conceal its abuses against the Iranian people, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is increasing pressure on the regime's shadow fleet. OFAC is targeting nine shadow fleet vessels and their respective owners or management firms that have collectively transported hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of Iranian oil and petroleum products to foreign markets. This revenue, which rightfully belongs to the Iranian people, is instead diverted to fund its regional terrorist proxies, weapons programs, and security services, instead of the basic economic services the Iranian people have bravely demanded.
  • More Than 3,000 People Died as Result of Armed Unrest in Iran - Foreign Minister Sputnik 23 Jan 2026 -- More than 3,000 people died as a result of the recent armed unrest that took place in Iran, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Friday.
  • Iran prosecutor general rejects Trump's claim on executions as 'completely false' Press TV 23 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Prosecutor General Mohammad Movahedi Azad dismissed President Donald Trump's claim that he stopped alleged executions in Iran, calling the assertion false and stressing the judiciary's independence from foreign pressure.
  • 'Confused clowns': Iran FM blasts Zelensky over call for aggression on Iran Press TV 23 Jan 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi lashed out at Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky over his call for foreign aggression against Iran, saying the world has had enough of "confused clowns."
  • Iran slams EU parliament's 'meddlesome, irresponsible' resolution on terrorist riots Press TV 23 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry has strongly condemned a "meddlesome and irresponsible" resolution issued by the European Parliament on recent foreign-backed terrorist attacks in the country, saying it was based on unfounded and incorrect claims.
  • UN Warns Of Unprecedented Protest Death Toll As US 'Armada' Heads To Iran RFE/RL 23 Jan 2026 -- US warships are heading toward Iran as Iranian officials defend a brutal suppression of anti-leadership protests that the United Nations is calling the deadliest crackdown by the Islamic republic on its people since it took power during the 1979 revolution.
  • Iran: After unprecedented violence, priority must be on gathering evidence to hold perpetrators to account, UN Fact-Finding Mission says OHCHR 23 Jan 2026 -- Following what appears to have been the deadliest crackdown by the Government of Iran against its people since the 1979 Revolution, the UN Fact-Finding Mission on Iran said the priority now must be on gathering evidence of alleged human rights violations and holding perpetrators to account.
  • UN Human Rights Council at Special Session condems crackdown in Iran Government of Iceland 23 Jan 2026 -- The United Nations Human Rights Council in Geneva today condemned the authorities in Iran for their recent crackdown on protesters in a resolution passed during a Special Session on the deteriorating human rights situation in Iran.
  • HRC - 39th Special Session on the human rights situation in the Islamic Republic of Iran - Core Group Statement Government of Iceland 23 Jan 2026
  • UN Human Rights Council Special Session to address the deteriorating Human Rights situation in the Islamic Republic of Iran, 23 January 2026 UK FCDO 23 Jan 2026 -- UK Statement for the Special Session to address the deteriorating Human Rights situation in the Islamic Republic of Iran. Delivered by the UK's Ambassador for Human Rights, Eleanor Sanders.
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna at the UN Human Rights Council: the Iranian regime must immediately end the repression of protesters Estonia MFA 23 Jan 2026 -- Today, 23 January, an emergency session of the UN Human Rights Council (HRC) was held in Geneva, focusing on the rapidly deteriorating human rights situation in Iran.
  • Iran slams UNHRC session as illegitimate, says no submission to foreign pressure Press TV 23 Jan 2026 -- Iran on Friday rejected a special session of the UN Human Rights Council as illegitimate, with its envoy in Geneva saying the country has never submitted—and will never submit—to foreign domination or pressure.
  • China hopes Iran will maintain national stability, all parties cherish peace and exercise restraint: FM on US claims of fleet heading toward Tehran Global Times 23 Jan 2026 -- In response to the claims by US President Donald Trump that the US is sending a massive fleet toward Iran, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday that China hopes Iran will maintain national stability, and hopes all parties will cherish peace, exercise restraint, and resolve differences through dialogue.
  • China urges all parties to exercise restraint as U.S. claims sending massive fleet toward Iran People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- China hopes that Iran will maintain national stability, and urges all parties to cherish peace, exercise restraint and resolve differences through dialogue, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson said on Friday.

  • Canada prime minister hits back at Trump's remarks Press TV 23 Jan 2026 -- Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has hit back at US President Donald Trump's assertion at the World Economic Forum in Davos that "Canada lives because of the United States."

  • The Daily Press Briefing (01/23/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 23 Jan 2026

  • Press conference with Federal Chancellor Merz and Prime Minister Meloni - United and strong in a new age of great powers Germany - Federal Government 23 Jan 2026

  • Japan's lower house formally dissolved for Feb. 8 election People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- Japan's House of Representatives was formally dissolved on Friday at the outset of the ordinary parliamentary session, with a general election set for Feb. 8.
  • Japan's cabinet approves decision to dissolve lower house People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's cabinet on Friday adopted a decision to dissolve the House of Representatives.

  • Oral conclusions drawn by President António Costa following the informal meeting of the members of the European Council of 22 January 2026 EC/CEU 23 Jan 2026

  • Milei says Argentina doesn't have to choose between US and China: media Global Times 23 Jan 2026 -- Argentine president Javier Milei said his country does not have to choose between the US and China as he appeared at the ongoing World Economic Forum in Davos, according to the Telegraph.

  • Rumen Radev: Like All Bulgarians, I believe that with Joint Efforts We Can Change Bulgaria President of the Republic of Bulgaria 23 Jan 2026

  • Maria Malmer Stenergard hosts meeting with Danish Minister for Foreign Affairs Lars Løkke Rasmussen Government Offices of Sweden 23 Jan 2026 -- On 22 January, Minister for Foreign Affairs Maria Malmer Stenergard received Danish Minister for Foreign Affairs Lars Løkke Rasmussen in Stockholm.

  • The President at informal European Council meeting: Europe has to remain united while strengthening transatlantic ties Lithuania MFA 23 Jan 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda participated in an informal European Council meeting focused on transatlantic relations.

  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Senator Booker Push for Accountability After Elections in Uganda US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 23 Jan 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH),Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Cory Booker (D-NJ), Ranking Member of the Subcommittee on Africa and Global Health Policy, released the following statement after the elections held in Uganda on January 15, 2026.
  • Risch Statement on Uganda Elections US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 23 Jan 2026 -- U.S. Senator Jim Risch (R-Idaho), chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the statement below on Uganda's recent elections.
  • Uganda: Statement by the Spokesperson on the general elections European External Action Service (EEAS) 23 Jan 2026 -- The European Union takes note of the official results announced by the Electoral Commission, according to which President Yoweri Museveni has been re-elected.

  • Xi congratulates To Lam on election as general secretary of Vietnam's CPV Central Committee People's Daily 23 Jan 2026 -- Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Communist Party of China Central Committee, on Friday congratulated To Lam on his election as general secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam (CPV) Central Committee.

  • World News in Brief: Iran in the Human Rights Council, Myanmar election 'fraud', migration chief in Cyprus, Mozambique flood update UN News 23 Jan 2026 -- Disturbing details continued to emerge on Friday about Iran's State-led crackdown against mass protests, while UN rights chief Volker Türk expressed deep concern that demonstrators could be executed.
